case_title: BRACAMONTES & VLASAK, P.C. VS. ELI SADDLER ET AL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (TRANSACTION ID # 15173079) FILED BY DEFENDANT SADDLER, ELI MCKINNEY, MISTY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ELI SADDLER, MISTY MCKINNEY'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT SEEKS TO RECOVER BECAUSE DEFENDANTS BREACHED THE RETAINER AGREEMENT AND FALSELY REPRESENTED THAT THEY WOULD PAY A 35% CONTINGENCY FEE. NONE OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE BASED ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND CCP 425.16 DOES NOT APPLY. THE MOTION IS FRIVOLOUS. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO $3,737.50 IN FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16(C)(1), ENFORCEABLE OF ENTRY OF FINAL JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-04-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BRACAMONTES & VLASAK, P.C.'S MOTION TO (1) COMPEL DEFENDANTS RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND (2) FOR ORDER THAT THE TRUTH OF ANY MATTERS SPECIFIED IN PLAINTIFFS REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION BE DEEMED ADMITTED; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND DEFENSE COUNSEL, ELI SADDLER IS GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES SET ONE, FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO, AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE. TO THE EXTENT THE MOTION SEEKS TO HAVE THE ADMISSIONS CONTAINED IN REQUEST FOR ADMISSION, SET ONE DEEMED ADMITTED, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS THE NOTICE OF MOTION DID NOT PROPERLY SET FORTH THIS REQUEST AS PART OF THE MOTION. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000.00, PAYABLE WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To (1) Compel Defendants Responses To Discovery And (2) For Order That The Truth Of Any Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants And Defense Counsel, Eli Saddler
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Schreiber of Bracamontes & Vlasak telephone number 415-835-6777 for Plaintiff.
Eli Saddler telephone number 415-335-6330 Defendant in Propria Persona.

Mr. Saddler stated that he was here on his own behalf, that his Attorney is in trial and that the parties would not agree to more time. Mr. Saddler also stated that he would request a continuance only if the Court strikes sanctions. The Court denied Mr. Saddler's request to strike sanctions. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF BRACAMONTES & VLASAK, P.C.'S Motion To (1) Compel Defendants Responses To Discovery And (2) For Order That The Truth Of Any Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants and Defense Counsel, Eli Saddler is GRANTED as to Special Interrogatories Set One, Form Interrogatories, Set Two, and Request for Production of Documents, Set One. To the extent the motion seeks to have the admissions contained in Request for Admission, Set One deemed admitted, the motion is DENIED as the Notice of Motion did not properly set forth this request as part of the motion. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,000.00, payable within 30 days of Notice of Entry of Order. This is a discovery matter and the court will not provide a court reporter. If the parties desire a reporter, then they shall meet and confer prior to the hearing and agree upon one. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2015 Set For Trial On Jun-06-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Aisa Villarosa, of Bracamontes & Vlasak, Tel. 415.835.6777, for plaintiffs;
Eli Saddler, in pro per, Tel. 415.335.6330, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ELI SADDLER, MISTY MCKINNEY'S Special Motion To Strike is denied. Plaintiff's complaint seeks to recover because defendants breached the retainer agreement and falsely represented that they would pay a 35% contingency fee. None of plaintiff's claims are based on protected activity and CCP 425.16 does not apply. The motion is frivolous. Plaintiff is entitled to $3,737.50 in fees and costs pursuant to CCP 425.16(c)(1), enforceable of entry of final judgment. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
